topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged, a bright, insistent chime against the quiet hum of my laptop. My heart gave a sudden, surprising leap. I blinked, rereading the screen. It was Professor Albright’s profile picture, and beneath it, a recent post about the history of Renaissance art. I leaned back in my chair, a wide, unguarded smile stretching across my face. I quickly scrolled through the feed, a bubbling excitement rising in my chest. This was… unexpected. I tapped the link, a giddy sense of triumph washing over me. I couldn't stop grinning; this felt like a secret victory.

[story 2]

I was halfway through my evening study session when I saw it—a notification from the academic platform. It was Professor Davies. My breath hitched. I stared at the screen, my fingers hovering over the 'view profile' button, a warmth spreading rapidly through my chest. A slow, delighted laugh escaped my lips. I felt a lightness in my step, as if a heavy weight had just been lifted. I clicked into his page, marveling at the casualness of his posts. I couldn't contain the sheer delight bubbling up; I felt an undeniable urge to share this wonderful piece of information with anyone.

[story 3]

My phone buzzed incessantly, pulling me from the monotony of my homework. It was a direct message from my calculus instructor. When I opened it, the sheer absurdity of the situation hit me, and I couldn't help the bright, unrestrained laugh that burst out of me. I sat bolt upright, my entire body vibrating with sheer pleasure. I scrolled through the messages, my eyes shining. It was a strange, wonderful feeling, like discovering a hidden treasure. I felt an energy surging through me, making my hands twitch with eagerness to respond.
